D&D Character Balance Tester

Friend Test Quickstart

One installer. One sample test. One D&D Beyond import path. Write down anything that feels confusing.

5minute start
EXE

Send this one file

release-current/DND-Character-Balance-Tester-Friend-Test-Setup.exe
SHA256: DBB3BDFD5596082A350BE437AAEB462A8098BB5BE6A4420E571DA12918A1DDD4
155 MB
installer
1

Double-click setup

Open DND-Character-Balance-Tester-Friend-Test-Setup.exe.

2

Pass Windows warning

Click More info, then Run anyway.

3

Load sample

Click Load sample so you can test without D&D Beyond first.

4

Begin simulation

Try the playable encounter and batch odds.

Windows may say Unknown Publisher because this is a self-signed personal test build. That is expected for now.

Start with the sample character before importing anything.

What to check first

  • Name
  • Class
  • Level
  • HP
  • AC
  • Weapons
  • Spells
  • Skills

Missing sheet data is useful feedback, especially for caster-heavy characters.

D&D Beyond Import Path

Public sheet required

Use the green-highlighted import box in the app.

Import Setup

Public

Set the D&D Beyond character to Public first.

Copy

Click Copy import bookmark code.

Bookmark

Paste it into a bookmark URL field.

Do not paste the import code into the address bar.
1

Open bookmarks

In Brave, Chrome, or Edge, press Ctrl + Shift + O.

2

Add bookmark

Name it Send to D&D Simulator.

3

Paste URL

Paste the copied code into the bookmark's URL box.

4

Click bookmark

Open the D&D Beyond sheet, then click the bookmark.

Play, Then Report What Felt Wrong

Tester notes matter

Playable mode should feel clear enough to replay, not just calculate results.

Best feedback

  • Where did you get stuck?
  • Which button wording was confusing?
  • Were weapons, spells, armor, and skills correct?
  • Did the playable encounter feel fun enough?
  • What did you expect that did not happen?

It does not install

Node.js, npm, Next.js, React, Git, MySQL, browser extensions, D&D Beyond credentials, services, or drivers.

It includes

Electron, Chromium, the built app files, the local import helper, and starter SRD monster data.
